     The Numbers Business Report

Lee Daniels’ The Butler (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $116,632,095Details
International Box Office $60,393,403
Worldwide Box Office $177,025,498
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $25,632,245
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $7,799,824
Total $33,432,069
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The story of a White House butler who served seven American presidents over three decades. The film traces the dramatic changes that swept American society during this time, from the civil rights movement to Vietnam and beyond, and how those changes affected this man's life and family.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$24,637,312 (21.1% of total gross)
Legs:4.73 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:65.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,933 opening theaters/3,330 max. theaters, 8.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $171,573,603

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 16th, 2013 (Wide) by Weinstein Co., released as Lee Daniels' The Butler
International Releases: September 5th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Portugal)
September 11th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Belgium)
October 10th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Germany)
October 11th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Sweden)
October 11th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Spain)
Video Release: January 14th, 2014 by Anchor Bay Home Entertainment, released as Lee Daniels' The Butler
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some violence and disturbing images, language, sexual material, thematic elements and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2283, 7/31/2013)
Running Time: 132 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Filming location (country): United States
Plot point: Addiction, African Americans, Assassination, Bigotry, Biography, Dysfunctional Family, Infidelity, Non-Chronological, Political, Sex Crimes, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: Apartheid, Upstairs / Downstairs
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Weinstein Company, Laura Ziskin Productions, Windy Hill Pictures, Follow Through Productions, Salamander Pictures, Pam Williams Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Full Financials

Synopsis

The story of a White House butler who served seven American presidents over three decades. The film traces the dramatic changes that swept American society during this time, from the civil rights movement to Vietnam and beyond, and how those changes affected this man's life and family.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$24,637,312 (21.1% of total gross)
Legs:4.73 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:65.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,933 opening theaters/3,330 max. theaters, 8.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $171,573,603

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 16th, 2013 (Wide) by Weinstein Co., released as Lee Daniels' The Butler
International Releases: September 5th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Portugal)
September 11th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Belgium)
October 10th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Germany)
October 11th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Sweden)
October 11th, 2013 (Wide), released as Lee Daniels' The Butler (Spain)
Video Release: January 14th, 2014 by Anchor Bay Home Entertainment, released as Lee Daniels' The Butler
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some violence and disturbing images, language, sexual material, thematic elements and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2283, 7/31/2013)
Running Time: 132 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Filming location (country): United States
Plot point: Addiction, African Americans, Assassination, Bigotry, Biography, Dysfunctional Family, Infidelity, Non-Chronological, Political, Sex Crimes, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: Apartheid, Upstairs / Downstairs
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Weinstein Company, Laura Ziskin Productions, Windy Hill Pictures, Follow Through Productions, Salamander Pictures, Pam Williams Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
